channel.title: Views on ink 🖋️
channel.description: Recent content in Views on ink 🖋️

item.title: Why you should increase delta before adding contracts when scaling options trades
item.description: Research from tastylive shows that increasing delta before adding contracts reduces tail risk by 70%+ compared to scaling through contract count. Here's why this matters for short premium traders.

item.title: Is the Indian stock market overvalued? Understanding the Buffett Indicator
item.description: A deep dive into the Buffett Indicator (Market Cap-to-GDP ratio) for Indian investors—what it measures, why it matters, and how to interpret current valuation levels.

item.title: A low-gamma straddle strategy for low IV environments
item.description: A straddle-like options strategy designed for low implied volatility environments with expected variance increases, featuring remarkably low gamma.

item.title: Hedging the downside with hedged futures
item.description: Why selling puts or strangles requires hedged futures on the downside rather than sold calls, due to IV expansion dynamics.

item.title: The problem with knowledge gained through guessing
item.description: A reflection on the anxiety of not trusting your own knowledge—why independent reasoning creates doubt, and how to build confidence in your thinking.

item.title: Another attempt at killing doom scrolling
item.description: A personal reflection on setting up VPN-based internet blocking to stop doom scrolling and reclaim attention.

item.title: How I calculate delta bands for Nifty 50 hedging
item.description: A practical method for calculating delta bands when hedging NIFTY 50 options positions using India VIX or weekly ATM IV.

item.title: Why Indian-made goods aren't actually 'cheap'
item.description: The next time someone says India built something for cheap, remember: it's not cheap. It's just measured in dollars. In purchasing power parity terms, we're right on par with everyone else.

item.title: Why the Indian stock market still has a massive runway for growth
item.description: Indian indices miss entire high-growth sectors like education, restaurants, gaming, and fintech. As these sectors eventually list, the market's investable universe will expand dramatically.
